# =========================================================
# INFO 6105 Final Project
# Impact of Sleep Duration on Academic Performance (GPA)
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Libraries
# -----------------------------
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(car)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)
library(pROC)
library(caret)
library(knitr)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Load data
# -----------------------------
# Replace with your actual file name
df <- read.csv("your_dataset.csv")

# Quick look
glimpse(df)
summary(df)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Data cleaning
# -----------------------------
# Keep only variables needed for project
df0 <- df %>%
  select(term_gpa, TotalSleepTime, cum_gpa, Zterm_units_ZofZ, study)

# Convert sleep to hours if stored in minutes
# Comment this out if your data is already in hours
df0 <- df0 %>%
  mutate(TotalSleepTime = TotalSleepTime / 60)

# Derived binary response
df0 <- df0 %>%
  mutate(high_gpa = if_else(term_gpa >= 3.0, 1, 0),
         high_gpa = factor(high_gpa, levels = c(0, 1)))

# Clean study as factor
df0 <- df0 %>%
  mutate(study = factor(study))

# Dataset for regression/logistic: listwise deletion
df_model <- df0 %>%
  drop_na(term_gpa, TotalSleepTime, cum_gpa, Zterm_units_ZofZ, study, high_gpa)

# Reduced sensitivity dataset without course load
df_reduced <- df0 %>%
  drop_na(term_gpa, TotalSleepTime, cum_gpa, study, high_gpa)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Summary statistics
# -----------------------------
summary_table <- df0 %>%
  summarise(
    mean_term_gpa = mean(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_term_gpa   = sd(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_term_gpa  = min(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_term_gpa  = max(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),

    mean_sleep = mean(TotalSleepTime, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_sleep   = sd(TotalSleepTime, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_sleep  = min(TotalSleepTime, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sleep  = max(TotalSleepTime, na.rm = TRUE),

    mean_cum_gpa = mean(cum_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_cum_gpa   = sd(cum_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_cum_gpa  = min(cum_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_cum_gpa  = max(cum_gpa, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

kable(summary_table, digits = 2)

# Missingness
missing_table <- sapply(df0, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
missing_pct <- round(100 * missing_table / nrow(df0), 2)
data.frame(variable = names(missing_table),
           missing_n = missing_table,
           missing_pct = missing_pct) %>%
  kable()

# -----------------------------
# 5. Exploratory visualizations
# -----------------------------
# Scatterplot
ggplot(df0, aes(x = TotalSleepTime, y = term_gpa)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Sleep Duration vs Term GPA",
       x = "Total Sleep Time (hours)",
       y = "Term GPA")

# Boxplot by university
ggplot(df0, aes(x = study, y = term_gpa)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Term GPA by University",
       x = "University",
       y = "Term GPA")

# High GPA by sleep categories
df0 <- df0 %>%
  mutate(sleep_cat = case_when(
    TotalSleepTime < 6 ~ "Low (<6h)",
    TotalSleepTime <= 8 ~ "Medium (6-8h)",
    TRUE ~ "High (>8h)"
  )) %>%
  mutate(sleep_cat = factor(sleep_cat, levels = c("Low (<6h)", "Medium (6-8h)", "High (>8h)")))

ggplot(df0, aes(x = sleep_cat, fill = high_gpa)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Proportion of High GPA Across Sleep Categories",
       x = "Sleep Category",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "High GPA")

# -----------------------------
# 6. Multiple Linear Regression
# -----------------------------
mlr_model <- lm(term_gpa ~ TotalSleepTime + cum_gpa + Zterm_units_ZofZ, data = df_model)
summary(mlr_model)

# R^2 and Adjusted R^2
glance(mlr_model)

# Coefficients table
tidy(mlr_model, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  kable(digits = 4)

# VIF
vif(mlr_model)

# Diagnostics
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(mlr_model)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# Additional diagnostic plots
# Residuals vs fitted
ggplot(data.frame(fitted = fitted(mlr_model), resid = resid(mlr_model)),
       aes(x = fitted, y = resid)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted", x = "Fitted", y = "Residuals")

# QQ plot
qqnorm(resid(mlr_model))
qqline(resid(mlr_model), col = "red")

# Scale-location
plot(mlr_model, which = 3)

# Sensitivity model without course load
mlr_reduced <- lm(term_gpa ~ TotalSleepTime + cum_gpa, data = df_reduced)
summary(mlr_reduced)

# -----------------------------
# 7. One-Way ANOVA
# -----------------------------
anova_model <- aov(term_gpa ~ study, data = df0)
summary(anova_model)

# Tukey HSD
tukey_res <- TukeyHSD(anova_model)
tukey_res

# Effect size eta^2
anova_table <- summary(anova_model)[[1]]
ss_between <- anova_table["study", "Sum Sq"]
ss_total <- sum(anova_table[, "Sum Sq"])
eta_sq <- ss_between / ss_total
eta_sq

# Assumption checks
# Normality of residuals
shapiro.test(residuals(anova_model))

# Equal variance
leveneTest(term_gpa ~ study, data = df0)

# Group summaries
df0 %>%
  group_by(study) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = mean(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(term_gpa, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 3)

# -----------------------------
# 8. Logistic Regression
# -----------------------------
logit_model <- glm(high_gpa ~ TotalSleepTime + cum_gpa + Zterm_units_ZofZ,
                   data = df_model,
                   family = binomial)

summary(logit_model)

# Odds ratios
exp(cbind(OR = coef(logit_model), confint(logit_model)))

# Predicted probabilities
df_model$pred_prob <- predict(logit_model, type = "response")

# Default threshold = 0.5
df_model$pred_class <- if_else(df_model$pred_prob >= 0.5, "1", "0") %>%
  factor(levels = c("0", "1"))

confusionMatrix(df_model$pred_class, df_model$high_gpa, positive = "1")

# ROC and AUC
roc_obj <- roc(response = df_model$high_gpa, predictor = df_model$pred_prob, levels = c("0", "1"))
plot(roc_obj, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve")
auc(roc_obj)

# Optional threshold exploration
thresholds <- seq(0.3, 0.7, by = 0.05)
threshold_eval <- map_df(thresholds, function(th) {
  pred <- if_else(df_model$pred_prob >= th, "1", "0") %>% factor(levels = c("0", "1"))
  cm <- confusionMatrix(pred, df_model$high_gpa, positive = "1")
  tibble(
    threshold = th,
    accuracy = cm$overall["Accuracy"],
    sensitivity = cm$byClass["Sensitivity"],
    specificity = cm$byClass["Specificity"],
    precision = cm$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  )
})

threshold_eval %>% kable(digits = 3)

# -----------------------------
# 9. Nonparametric Analysis
# -----------------------------
kruskal.test(term_gpa ~ study, data = df0)

# Optional Spearman if you examine monotonic sleep-GPA association
cor.test(df0$TotalSleepTime, df0$term_gpa, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")

# -----------------------------
# 10. Save key outputs
# -----------------------------
write.csv(as.data.frame(tidy(mlr_model, conf.int = TRUE)),
          "mlr_coefficients.csv", row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(as.data.frame(threshold_eval),
          "logistic_threshold_eval.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Session info for reproducibility
sessionInfo()
